TemplateParameter.Name: Level
TemplateParameter.Description: Select an aggregation level in your metric set to define your metrics.

TemplateParameter.Name: EventDefinition
TemplateParameter.Description: Write a base-level boolean event condition (example: `EventName == “click”`) or the name of a boolean custom column (example: `IsClick`).

TemplateParameter.Name: EventNameForDisplay
TemplateParameter.Description: Use a friendly nickname for the event definition to display in scorecards.

TemplateParameter.Name: ColorScheme
TemplateParameter.Description: Write 'Default' if the goal is to increase the metric value; 'Inverse' if the goal is to decrease the metric value; “Neutral” if the goal direction depends on the experiment.

ComputationTemplate.TemplateDescription:
The purpose of the event definition template is to create indicator and value metrics for an event captured in your telemetry.
The proportion metric will create a binary condition at your aggregation level's record of events of whether the event occurred, and report a proportion of events in each of your variant groups in the scorecard.
The count metric will first count all events occurring in an aggregation level's record of events, and report an average count in each of your variant groups in the scorecard.

Statistic.Name: ${Level}Proportion_${EventNameForDisplay}
Statistic.Description:
The proportion of all ${Level} which had the ${EventNameForDisplay} event. This event is defined as:

${EventDefinition}

Note: This metric considers all ${Level} assigned to the experiment.

Internal name: ${Level}Proportion_${EventNameForDisplay}

Statistic.Name: ${Level}Value_${EventNameForDisplay}
Statistic.Description:
The average count of all ${Level} of ${EventNameForDisplay} event. This event is defined as:

${EventDefinition}

Note: This metric considers all ${Level}s assigned to the experiment.

Internal name: ${Level}Value_${EventNameForDisplay}

TemplateParameter.Name: Level
TemplateParameter.Description: Select an aggregation level in your metric set to define your metrics.

TemplateParameter.Name: TimeAsDouble
TemplateParameter.Description: Use an existing base-level custom column that converts the timestamp to a double column in seconds. Example definition: new DateTimeOffset(Timestamp).ToUnixTimeSeconds()

TemplateParameter.Name: FirstAction
TemplateParameter.Description: For the first action in your funnel, write a base-level boolean event condition (example: `EventName == 'click'`) or the name of a boolean custom column (example: `IsClick`)

TemplateParameter.Name: FirstActionForDisplay
TemplateParameter.Description: Use a friendly nickname for the first action definition to display in scorecards.

TemplateParameter.Name: SecondAction
TemplateParameter.Description: For the second action in your funnel, write a base-level boolean event condition (example: `EventName == 'purchase'`) or the name of a boolean custom column (example: `IsPurchase`)

TemplateParameter.Name: SecondActionForDisplay
TemplateParameter.Description: Use a friendly nickname for the second action definition to display in scorecards.

TemplateParameter.Name: ColorScheme
TemplateParameter.Description: Write 'Default' if the goal is to increase the metric value; 'Inverse' if the goal is to decrease the metric value; 'Neutral' if the goal direction depends on the experiment.

ComputationTemplate.TemplateDescription: The purpose of the funnel definition template is to create an indicator metric for a "second" action or event captured in your telemetry conditional on the action occurring in the population who had a "first" action or event and specific to second events that came after the first event. The metric created is a conditional proportion of aggregation levels that have the second action given the level had the first action before the second action or not at all.

Statistic.Name: ${Level}Proportion_${SecondActionForDisplay}_After_${FirstActionForDisplay}
Statistic.Description: The proportion of all ${Level} which had the ${FirstActionForDisplay} FirstAction, then the ${SecondActionForDisplay} SecondAction.

TemplateParameter.Name: Level
TemplateParameter.Description: Select an aggregation level in your metric set to define your metrics.

TemplateParameter.Name: FirstAction
TemplateParameter.Description: For the first action in your funnel, write a base-level boolean event condition (example: `EventName == 'click'`) or the name of a boolean custom column (example: `IsClick`)

TemplateParameter.Name: FirstActionForDisplay
TemplateParameter.Description: Use a friendly nickname for the first action definition to display in scorecards.

TemplateParameter.Name: SecondAction
TemplateParameter.Description: For the second action in your funnel, write a base-level boolean event condition (example: `EventName == 'purchase'`) or the name of a boolean custom column (example: `IsPurchase`)

TemplateParameter.Name: SecondActionForDisplay
TemplateParameter.Description: Use a friendly nickname for the second action definition to display in scorecards.

TemplateParameter.Name: ColorScheme
TemplateParameter.Description: Write 'Default' if the goal is to increase the metric value; 'Inverse' if the goal is to decrease the metric value; 'Neutral' if the goal direction depends on the experiment.

ComputationTemplate.TemplateDescription: The purpose of the ratio definition template is to create indicator and ratio metrics for a "second" action or event captured in your telemetry conditional on the action occurring in the population who had a "first" action or event. No condition checking the time-ordering of events is implemented. The two metrics created are a conditional proportion of the second action occurring > 0 times per first action occurring > 0 times, and a ratio of second action total counts to first action total counts.

foray:Statistic.Name: ${Level}_Proportion_${FirstActionForDisplay}_${SecondActionForDisplay}
foray:Statistic.foray:Description: Percent, among all ${Level} where ${FirstActionForDisplay} is taken at least 1x, where ${SecondActionForDisplay} is also taken at least 1x by the same ${Level}.

foray:Statistic.Name: ${Level}AvgRatio_${FirstActionForDisplay}_${SecondActionForDisplay}
foray:Statistic.foray:Description: Average ratio of ${SecondActionForDisplay} per ${FirstActionForDisplay}, among all ${Level} where ${FirstActionForDisplay} is taken at least 1x.

TemplateParameter.Name: Level
TemplateParameter.Description: Select an aggregation level in your metric set to define your metrics.

TemplateParameter.Name: TimeAsDouble
TemplateParameter.Description: Use an existing base-level custom column that converts the timestamp to a double column in seconds. Example definition: new DateTimeOffset(Timestamp).ToUnixTimeSeconds()

TemplateParameter.Name: Day
TemplateParameter.Description: Use an existing base-level custom column that converts the timestamp to a day-month-year string column. Example definition: Timestamp.ToString('dd-MM-yyyy')

ComputationTemplate.TemplateDescription:
The purpose of the retention definition template is to create indicator metrics for an aggregation level's returned activity during the experiment period.
The '1 week retention' metrics will count the proportion of aggregation levels who returned after 7 days from the first time visiting the product.
The '% 2-day Engaged' metric measures the proportion of aggregation levels having activity in 2 different days.
The 'Active Days/UU' metric measures the value metric of the average number of days aggregation levels have activity.

Statistic.Name: DaysPer${Level}
Statistic.Description: Average numbers of days with active use. Calculated as (total # of days ${Level}s are considered active)/(total # of ${Level}s).

Statistic.Name: Percent2DEngaged${Level}s
Statistic.Description: Proportion of ${Level}s who are considered engaged ${Level}s (had on 2 different days).

Statistic.Name: OneWeekRetention
Statistic.Description: Proportion of ${Level}s who came back and engaged later than seven days after they were first engaged.

TemplateParameter.Name: Level
TemplateParameter.Description: Select an aggregation level in your metric set to define your metrics.

TemplateParameter.Name: TimeAsDouble
TemplateParameter.Description: Use an existing base-level custom column that converts the timestamp to a double column in seconds. Example definition: new DateTimeOffset(Timestamp).ToUnixTimeSeconds()

TemplateParameter.Name: Day
TemplateParameter.Description: Use an existing base-level custom column that converts the timestamp to a day-month-year string column. Example definition: Timestamp.ToString('dd-MM-yyyy')

TemplateParameter.Name: ScopeDefinition
TemplateParameter.Description: To measure engagement on a particular scope of your logs, write a base-level boolean scoping condition (example: `routeView.Contains('homepage')`) or the name of a base-level boolean custom column (example: `IsHomePage`)

TemplateParameter.Name: ScopeNameForDisplay
TemplateParameter.Description: Use a friendly nickname for the second action definition to display in scorecards.

ComputationTemplate.TemplateDescription:
The purpose of the engagement definition template is to create proportion metrics for an aggregation level's revisiting a scope (or feature) during the experiment period conditional on the aggregation level having any activity in a certain scope of your UX.
The "1 week retention" metrics will count the proportion of aggregation levels in a scope who returned after 7 days from the first time visiting the scope. There are two versions of this metric:
*"1 week Retention" will report the filtered retention for activity in the scope among all aggregation levels in the experiment - users without any activity in the scope contribute 0 to the metric*"1 week Active User Retention" will report the conditional retention for activity in the scope only among with any activity in the scope - aggregation levels without activity are not in the metric.

The "1 week returned" metrics will count the proportion of aggregation levels in a scope who returned after 7 days from their first time visiting the product - which may be earlier than visiting the scope. Similarly, there are two versions of this metric:
* "1 week Returned" will report the filtered retention for activity in the scope among all aggregation levels in the experiment - aggregation levels without any activity in the scope contribute 0 to the metric* "1 week Active User Returned" will report the conditional retention for activity in the scope only among with any activity in the scope - aggregation levels without activity are not in the metric.
The "% 2-day Engaged" metric measures the proportion of aggregation levels having activity in the scope in 2 different days.
The "Active Days/UU" metric measures the conditional value metric of the average number of days users have activity in a given scope.

Statistic.Name: ${ScopeNameForDisplay}DaysPer${Level}
Statistic.Description: Average numbers of days ${Level}s actively use ${ScopeNameForDisplay}. Calculated as (total # of days ${Level}s are considered active)/(total # of ${Level}s).

Statistic.Name: ${ScopeNameForDisplay}_Percent2DEngaged${Level}s
Statistic.Description: Proportion of ${Level}s who are considered engaged ${ScopeNameForDisplay} ${Level}s (had on 2 different days).

Statistic.Name: ${ScopeNameForDisplay}OneWeekRetention
Statistic.Description: Proportion of ${ScopeNameForDisplay} ${Level}s who came back and engaged later than seven days after they were first engaged.
Statistic.Definition:
STAVG(
(double?)MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity)) != double.PositiveInfinity ?
(MAX<${Level}>(
MAX<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.NegativeInfinity)) - MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity))
) / 86400.0 > 7.0 ? 1.0 : 0.0 )
: 0.0
)

Statistic.Name: ${ScopeNameForDisplay}OneWeekReturned
Statistic.Description: Proportion of ${ScopeNameForDisplay} ${Level}s who came back later than seven days after they were first engaged.
Statistic.Definition:
STAVG(
(double?)MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity)) != double.PositiveInfinity ?
(MAX<${Level}>(
MAX<${Level}>(${TimeAsDouble}) - MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity))
) / 86400.0 > 7.0 ? 1.0 : 0.0 )
: 0.0
)

Statistic.Name: ${ScopeNameForDisplay}OneWeekActive${Level}Retention
Statistic.Description: Proportion of active ${ScopeNameForDisplay} ${Level}s who came back and engaged later than seven days after they were first engaged.
Statistic.Definition:
STAVG(
(double?)MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity)) != double.PositiveInfinity ?
(MAX<${Level}>(
MAX<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.NegativeInfinity)) - MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity))
) / 86400.0 > 7.0 ? 1.0 : 0.0 )
: (double?)null
)

Statistic.Name: ${ScopeNameForDisplay}OneWeekActive${Level}Returned
Statistic.Description: Proportion of active ${ScopeNameForDisplay} ${Level}s who came back later than seven days after they were first engaged.
Statistic.Definition:
STAVG(
(double?)MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity)) != double.PositiveInfinity ?
(MAX<${Level}>(
MAX<${Level}>(${TimeAsDouble}) - MIN<${Level}>((${ScopeDefinition} ? ${TimeAsDouble} : double.PositiveInfinity))
) / 86400.0 > 7.0 ? 1.0 : 0.0 )
: (double?)null
)
